flags.note: enToDe gives 'Chips (full)' / 'Pommes (partial)', but the item teaches BrE 'chips' = hot fried potato (definition d: 'food you eat hot with fish'; carrier 'fish and chips'). In German 'Chips' = the cold bag snack (crisps), so the full-tier gloss reads against the intended hot-fries meaning while the meaning-accurate 'Pommes' is only partial. Defensible as the conventional loanword answer, but the tiers arguably sit the wrong way round for the BrE sense.
